claim_boundary: This contract binds the exact product-truth comparator and governance obligations for the Gate F narrow wedge only. It does not open Gate F or widen beyond the local verifier mode.
explicit_non_claims: This contract does not open Gate F.
This contract does not imply Gate G, re-audit, or enterprise rollout.
This contract does not treat Gate D/E success as automatic product entitlement.
This contract does not widen beyond local_verifier_mode.

gate_f_comparator_definition.lawful_comparator_win_definition: A Gate F narrow wedge claim is lawful only when the local verifier mode can be run from the governed wrapper, returns the declared receipt/proof surfaces, and stays inside the explicit single-tenant support boundary.

gate_f_governance_bundle.rollback_rule: Any contradiction, governance drift, or support-boundary violation demotes the wedge back below admissibility.

predicate_to_evidence_map.failure_rule: If either D clear or E open is false, Gate F is fail-closed.
predicate_to_evidence_map.satisfaction_rule: Gate D cleared and Gate E open are both true on the successor line.

predicate_to_evidence_map.failure_rule: If local_verifier_mode is missing or widened, the wedge contract is invalid.
predicate_to_evidence_map.satisfaction_rule: local_verifier_mode exists and remains bounded to E1-style same-host use.

predicate_to_evidence_map.failure_rule: Any training, runtime cutover, or multi-tenant claim invalidates the contract.
predicate_to_evidence_map.satisfaction_rule: No training, no runtime cutover, single-tenant-only posture.

predicate_to_evidence_map.failure_rule: Missing wrapper command or receipt refs blocks Gate F admissibility.
predicate_to_evidence_map.satisfaction_rule: The wrapper declares verify and detached PASS/FAIL entrypoints and receipt refs.
